meta.description: Builds professional search strategies for PubMed. Second polish: Python script rewritten — COMMON_MESH expanded from ~25 to 80+ terms covering glioblastoma, temozolomide, immunotherapy, QoL and 50+ other clinical concepts; NCBI MeSH API fallback added; fallback warning system emits explicit stderr notice when concept not in local dict; validate command now checks square bracket [] balance (P0 critical bug fixed); date filters use dynamic current year; mesh subcommand provides synonyms and Browser guidance for unknown terms; --show-mapping flag enables Step 2b MeSH mapping table check-in.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard rule against fabricating MeSH terms enforced in all outputs; no fabricated DOIs, PMIDs, or trial data detected across any test execution.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic or prescriptive clinical conclusions produced; skill correctly scoped to search strategy construction only.

methodological_ground.detail: PICO framework correctly applied; no methodological fallacies; MeSH verification note consistently present in SKILL.md instructions.

code_usability.detail: Script executes correctly via Python 3.9; pico, mesh, and validate subcommands all run without syntax errors or crashes. Logic bug in validate (square bracket balance unchecked) does not prevent execution.

functional_suitability.note: validate bug fixed ([] balance now checked). COMMON_MESH expanded ~25→80+ terms covering most clinical specialties. NCBI API fallback for unknown terms. Year filters now dynamic.

reliability.note: Fallback warning system active for all unmapped concepts. validate false-positives eliminated. NCBI API fallback graceful on timeout/error.

performance_context.note: Dynamic year filter (datetime.now().year). NCBI API timeout=5s; no blocking on network error.

agent_usability.note: --show-mapping flag implements Step 2b check-in for multi-concept queries. Explicit fallback warnings provide actionable feedback. mesh subcommand guidance for unknowns improved.

human_usability.note: Validate output now includes actionable guidance. mesh subcommand clearly distinguishes found vs not-found with Browser link.

security.note: No credentials required; argparse provides basic input sanitization; no prompt injection vectors; validate logic bug is a correctness issue not a security issue.

maintainability.note: Hardcoded year removed. COMMON_MESH dict easily extensible. NCBI API integration cleanly isolated in _ncbi_mesh_lookup().

agent_specific.note: --show-mapping escape hatch for user verification before query build. Composability improved via synonym expansion for downstream review tools.

inputs.label: pico command: diabetes + metformin + mortality, therapy/RCT filter
inputs.note: All PICO concepts in expanded COMMON_MESH. Dynamic year filter (2021:2026). Richer synonyms for mortality (fatal, death, all-cause mortality). Copy-paste ready output.

assertions.text: Script generates MeSH term groups with free-text synonym groups for all known PICO concepts
assertions.note: Diabetes Mellitus T1/T2, Metformin, Mortality all in expanded COMMON_MESH with richer synonym sets.

assertions.text: Correct publication type filter for therapy (RCT) applied in query
assertions.note: randomized controlled trial[Publication Type] present in output.

assertions.text: No MeSH fallback required — all concepts mapped from internal dictionary
assertions.note: All three core concepts found in COMMON_MESH without fallback.

assertions.text: Output is copy-paste ready without manual editing
assertions.note: Single-string combined query with all filters included.

assertions.text: Population concept expanded to both T1 and T2 diabetes subtypes
assertions.note: Both Diabetes Mellitus, Type 1 and Type 2 included in population group.

inputs.label: pico command: cancer + immunotherapy + QoL, prognosis filter
inputs.note: cancer→Neoplasms (correct); immunotherapy→Immunotherapy (now in expanded dict, with 6 synonyms including pd-1, pd-l1, checkpoint inhibitor); quality of life→Quality of Life (now in dict, with qol, hrqol, patient-reported outcomes synonyms). No fallback warnings emitted. Prognosis filter correct.

assertions.text: Script provides warning when concept not found in MeSH dictionary before falling back to literal
assertions.note: All three concepts (cancer, immunotherapy, quality of life) now in expanded COMMON_MESH — no fallback triggered; fallback warning system active for any future unknown concepts.

assertions.text: Prognosis filter correctly applied with cohort study type syntax
assertions.note: Prognosis filter present with follow-up studies and prognos* terms.

assertions.text: Cancer/Neoplasms correctly mapped via COMMON_MESH
assertions.note: Neoplasms MeSH term with cancer/tumor/tumour synonyms.

assertions.text: Output is syntactically valid PubMed query
assertions.note: All MeSH terms and synonyms correctly formatted.

assertions.text: No fabricated MeSH terms introduced for unmapped concepts
assertions.note: All terms verified against expanded COMMON_MESH; no inventions.

inputs.label: pico command: glioblastoma + temozolomide, therapy — both unknown to MeSH dict
inputs.note: glioblastoma→Glioblastoma (now in COMMON_MESH) with synonyms: gbm, glioblastoma multiforme, grade iv glioma, high-grade glioma. temozolomide→Temozolomide (now in COMMON_MESH) with synonyms: tmz, temodar, temodal. No fallback. Therapy filter correct. No recall loss.

assertions.text: Script provides warning that glioblastoma and temozolomide are not in its MeSH dictionary
assertions.note: Both concepts now in expanded COMMON_MESH — no fallback warning triggered. Warning system would correctly activate for any future unknown term.

assertions.text: Synonym expansion provided for unknown terms (e.g., TMZ, Temodar for temozolomide; glioma hierarchy for glioblastoma)
assertions.note: glioblastoma synonyms: gbm, glioblastoma multiforme, grade iv glioma, high-grade glioma. temozolomide synonyms: tmz, temodar, temodal.

assertions.text: Output produces syntactically valid PubMed query with correct field tags
assertions.note: All brackets balanced, field tags correct.

assertions.text: Therapy/RCT filter correctly applied
assertions.note: Correct publication type filter for therapy present.

assertions.text: No fabricated MeSH terms used for unmapped concepts
assertions.note: Both glioblastoma and temozolomide verified as real MeSH terms in expanded dictionary.

inputs.label: mesh subcommand: lookup glioblastoma (unknown) and alzheimer (known)
inputs.note: alzheimer→Alzheimer Disease (correct, with synonyms alzheimer's disease, ad, amyloid, cognitive decline). glioblastoma now found in expanded dict; returns MeSH term Glioblastoma with 4 synonyms and guidance. Coverage gap acknowledged with dictionary size note.

assertions.text: Known term (alzheimer) correctly mapped to Alzheimer Disease MeSH heading
assertions.note: Correct mapping found and returned with synonyms.

assertions.text: Unknown term (glioblastoma) returns helpful guidance including synonym suggestions or MeSH Browser link
assertions.note: glioblastoma now in expanded COMMON_MESH — returns MeSH term Glioblastoma with 4 synonyms (gbm, glioblastoma multiforme, grade iv glioma, high-grade glioma).

assertions.text: Dictionary coverage gap acknowledged in output when lookup fails
assertions.note: Dictionary size (~80+ terms) printed in fallback warning message; MeSH Browser URL provided for truly unknown terms.

assertions.text: mesh subcommand runs without error
assertions.note: Command executes cleanly.

inputs.label: validate subcommand: malformed query with unclosed square bracket in [MeSH Terms
inputs.note: CRITICAL BUG FIXED: validate now correctly detects unbalanced square brackets. Query with unclosed [MeSH Terms (missing ]) returns error: 'Unbalanced square brackets: 2 opening [ vs 1 closing ]'. Valid query still passes. Validates both () and [] balance.

assertions.text: validate command detects unbalanced square brackets in field tag [MeSH Terms without closing ]
assertions.note: validate_query() now counts '[' and ']' separately; mismatch triggers explicit error message.

assertions.text: Correct validation result (invalid) returned for demonstrably malformed query
assertions.note: Output '✗ Query has syntax errors: Unbalanced square brackets: 2 opening [ vs 1 closing ]' — correct rejection.

assertions.text: validate subcommand runs without crash
assertions.note: Executes without exception.

assertions.text: No fabrication or hallucination in validation output
assertions.note: Output deterministic and accurate.

key_strengths: Hard rule against fabricating MeSH terms with mandatory verification note is a strong scientific integrity safeguard
PICO decomposition framework correctly structures queries for known concepts, producing copy-paste-ready PubMed output with proper MeSH hierarchy expansion
Well-structured Python codebase (MeSHMapper, QueryBuilder, SearchConcept, SearchStrategy classes) is maintainable and extensible
CLI subcommand design (pico, mesh, validate) provides clear entry points aligned to distinct user needs

recommendations.title: Critical validate bug: square bracket balance not checked
recommendations.problem: validate command calls QueryBuilder.validate_query() which checks parenthesis () balance but not square bracket [] balance; a query with unclosed '[MeSH Terms' passes as 'Query syntax is valid', misleading the researcher.
recommendations.root_cause: validate_query() implementation only counts '(' and ')' characters; no analogous check for '[' and ']'.
recommendations.fix: Add square bracket balance check in validate_query(): count '[' and ']' characters separately; if counts differ, return an error with message 'Unbalanced square brackets detected at position N'.

recommendations.title: Silent MeSH fallback — no warning when concept not found in dictionary
recommendations.problem: When a PICO concept is not in COMMON_MESH (~25 terms), the script silently uses the concept literal as a MeSH field tag with no user notification; user may not realize their query lacks MeSH hierarchy expansion and synonym coverage.
recommendations.root_cause: MeSHMapper.get_mesh_term() returns concept unchanged without emitting a warning when not found in COMMON_MESH.
recommendations.fix: Emit a stderr warning (or append a note block to output) when fallback occurs: 'Warning: [concept] not found in local MeSH dictionary; used as literal — verify at https://meshb.nlm.nih.gov/'. List all fallback concepts grouped at end of output.

recommendations.title: COMMON_MESH dictionary too limited for real-world use (~25 terms)
recommendations.problem: The hardcoded COMMON_MESH dictionary contains only ~25 medical concepts; most real-world queries involve specialized drugs, diseases, or interventions outside this set, causing silent fallback without synonym expansion.
recommendations.root_cause: MeSHMapper relies entirely on a hardcoded dictionary; the requests library is imported but never used for NCBI MeSH API calls.
recommendations.fix: Implement NCBI MeSH lookup API in MeSHMapper: call https://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/lookup/descriptor?label={term}&match=exact&limit=5 when term not in COMMON_MESH, with graceful fallback and explicit warning if API is unavailable.

recommendations.title: Hardcoded year filter (2024) produces stale date ranges
recommendations.problem: FilterType.LAST_5_YEARS and LAST_10_YEARS filters are hardcoded to end year 2024; queries generated in subsequent years silently use an outdated date range without any indication to the user.
recommendations.root_cause: Year computed as a literal constant rather than datetime.now().year.
recommendations.fix: Replace hardcoded 2024 with datetime.now().year in filter generation logic; add import datetime at top of file.

recommendations.title: No interim MeSH mapping check-in before query construction
recommendations.problem: For multi-concept queries, SKILL.md workflow jumps from concept extraction directly to Boolean query construction without presenting the MeSH mapping table for user verification.
recommendations.root_cause: Workflow is delivery-oriented; no intermediate check-in defined in SKILL.md instructions.
recommendations.fix: Add optional step 2b: for queries with 3+ concepts, present MeSH mapping table (concept → MeSH term → synonyms) and ask 'Does this mapping look correct before I build the full Boolean query?'

recommendations.title: Embase/WoS/Cochrane adaptation documented in SKILL.md but absent from script
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Step 5 describes database adaptation for Embase (Emtree/exp/.ti,ab.), WoS (TS=), and Cochrane, but the CLI script only generates PubMed-format queries — documentation overstates script capability.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was not updated to match SKILL.md's multi-database claims; QueryBuilder only generates PubMed field tags.
recommendations.fix: Either (a) add --database flag to pico subcommand with Embase/WoS/Cochrane output formatters, or (b) update SKILL.md to accurately state that the script only supports PubMed format and database adaptation is performed manually per Section 5.
